title: Learn Accessibility lesson by lesson.
description: Practise wheelchair accessibility, assistance dogs, disability awareness, ramps, restraints, and passenger support.

lessons.title: Wheelchair Safety
lessons.intro: Wheelchair orientation, restraints, ramps, doors, lighting and vehicle requirements.
lessons.memoryTrick: Wheelchair safety = position, secure, light, access.

lessons.title: Assistance Passengers
lessons.intro: How to assist blind, deaf and mobility-impaired passengers professionally.
commonMistakes: Failing to communicate clearly with deaf or blind passengers

lessons.title: Guide and Assistance Dogs
lessons.intro: Harness colours, service animals, and mandatory carriage rules.

lessons.title: Grants and Training
lessons.intro: Wheelchair Accessible Vehicle grants, training, evidence and compliance.
lessons.memoryTrick: Grant means evidence, training and ongoing compliance.

lessons.title: Final Mixed Accessibility Test
lessons.memoryTrick: Passenger dignity and vehicle safety come first.
commonMistakes: Mixing vehicle standards with passenger-service rules

questions.questionText: Which way must the wheelchair and the person travelling in it be facing?
options: Either forwards facing or backwards facing
questions.explanation: Recorded from the uploaded Accessibility question bank. Correct answer is based on the yellow highlighted answer.
questions.memoryTip: Read the accessibility requirement carefully and choose the passenger-safe option.
questions.commonMistake: Do not guess from normal taxi rules; wheelchair accessible vehicles and assistance passengers have specific rules.

questions.questionText: When can the NTA withdraw the Wheelchair Vehicle grant?
options: If the recipient ceases to carry on any services
If the recipient ceases to make the wheelchair accessible SPSV available for wheelchair customers
If the recipient commits a breach of any terms or conditions, and fails to rectify such breach within 10 Working Days after written notice

questions.questionText: When there is a Wheelchair Accessible passenger how many passenger must you accommodate?
options: At least one person in a wheelchair and four other adult passenger
Atleast one person in a wheelchair and three other adult passenger
At least one person in a wheelchair and one other adult passenger
At least one person in a wheelchair and two other adult passenger

questions.questionText: What do you do when you arrive to pick up a blind person?
options: When you pick up a customer who is blind or vision impaired, introduce yourself and use the customer’s name, if you know it. Instead, at the time of booking, you could arrange to do something like give them two rings on their telephone when you arrive and then escort them to your vehicle.
Sounds your horn when you arrive.
Wait in your car for them to enter.
